mdx.body:
import { Paragraph } from "../../../src/components/Mdx/textComponents" import CodeWrapper from "../../../src/components/Mdx/CodeWrapper" # Text #### The responsive text classes The text classes that have to do with size are resposive by default. While it is possible to change what is responsive or not (see the [Customize](/customize/) section) I figured that a good rule of thumb is to make things that have sizes responsive, so that includes `font-size`, `line-height`, and `letter-spacing` classes. ## Font Size Font size classes are prefixed by `text-` as follows: | Class Example | CSS rule | Example | | :------------ | :------------------- | :--------------------------------------------------- | | text-0 | font-size: 0; | | | text-xs | font-size: 0.375rem; | | | text-sm | font-size: 0.675rem; | | | text-md | font-size: 0.875rem; | | | text-base | font-size: 1rem; | | | text-lg | font-size: 1.25rem; | | | text-xl | font-size: 1.5rem; | | | text-2xl | font-size: 1.75rem; | | | text-3xl | font-size: 3rem; | | | text-4xl | font-size: 4rem; | | | text-5xl | font-size: 5rem; | | Font sizes respond to both Pseudo-Classes as well as responsive breakpoints. For Pseudo class examples, feel free to follow this link [Font size Pseudo Classes](/docs/additional-classes/?anchor=font-size/) ### Example Anyway let's look at a couple responsive examples. Here's an example that changes with the default breakpoints from mobile to large. If you're in a browser you can resize the window to see it working, on small widths you'll have to close the sidebar:
The html for the size-changing paragraph is pretty straight forward: ```HTML

Resize the screen to see this font size change!

``` ## Line Height Like text size, line height is also a responsive class, and the line height default classes are as follows (font size is xl): | Class Example | CSS rule | Example | | :-------------- | :------------------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | line-height-0 | line-height: 0; | | | line-height-sm | line-height: 1em; | | | line-height-md | line-height: 1.2em; | | | line-height-lg | line-height: 1.5em; | | | line-height-xl | line-height: 1.75em; | | | line-height-2xl | line-height: 2em; | | ### Example Like above, on small widths for this example you'll have to close the sidebar. But here's a block of text that changes font sizes and line heights from small displays to large. Note that font sizes are in `rem` while line heights are in `em` which I think works better:
## Example I kept the same font size classes as the font size exmaple, but added break points for the line height classes: ```HTML

Resize the screen to see this font size change!

``` ## Letter Spacing The final font class that is by default responsive is the letter spacing. Note that like line height, letter spacing is also in `em` while font is in `rem` in order for it to work better: | Class Example | CSS rule | Example | | :----------------- | :----------------------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | letter-spacing-sm | letter-spacing: 0.006em; | | | letter-spacing-md | letter-spacing: 0.013em; | | | letter-spacing-lg | letter-spacing: 0.025em; | | | letter-spacing-xl | letter-spacing: 0.044em; | | | letter-spacing-2xl | letter-spacing: 0.063em; | | | letter-spacing-3xl | letter-spacing: 0.125em; | | The values are very close together since small changes can make the difference between subtle and overdone in my opioion. Of course, any of these values can be added/removed/changed/renamed in the `_variables.scss` file. The text in these docs for example, has a letter spacing of `sm` making it just a nudge bigger than the default, which I think is just a little bit more readable for this font. ### Example Anyway, keeping with the same example as above, but this time the letter spacing changes with the line height and font size for the various default breakpoints:
Anyhow, those are the responsive text/font classes. Happy coding!
